title: Use-after-free of an `os.DirEntry` string under OOM (eval-loop stackref over-close; `DirEntry_dealloc`, `posixmodule.c:16199`)
description: Under OOM, the eval loop's exception_unwind handler (Python/generated_cases.c.h:13853) pops and PyStackRef_XCLOSEs every value-stack slot of a frame whose MemoryError is unhandled; one slot references a live os.DirEntry's path string (created at DirEntry_from_posix_info, posixmodule.c:16747, while building list(os.scandir(d))), so it is freed while entry->path still points to it. The later DirEntry_dealloc Py_XDECREFs the dangling field (posixmodule.c:16200) -> _Py_NegativeRefcount on debug/JIT builds, SIGSEGV on the release upstream build (assert compiled out under NDEBUG). A genuine memory-safety use-after-free; the bug is in the eval-loop stackref accounting (same class as OOM-0005), not in os.scandir (DirEntry is the incidental victim).
notes: FIRST bug found by the new OOM Phase-4 stateful sequences (--oom-seq); vehicle zoneinfo._tzpath. A crash the single-call fail-forever harness cannot reach: it always trips the shallow OOM-0003 (code_dealloc/_co_unique_id) first, whereas the windowed sequence runs past that shallow crash (its preceding step warms the code objects) to reach this deeper UAF. Reduced out of zoneinfo to a bare recursive os.scandir under the set_nomemory sweep; deterministic on ft_debug_asan, GIL on and off, window 1 or fail-forever. A warm-up walk is load-bearing (else OOM-0003 masks it). Victim = a DirEntry name string freed while the DirEntry is still live (confirmed UAF: freed memory, debug deadbyte 0xdd, at the second Py_XDECREF). DirEntry-specific (a generic non-DirEntry recursion does not reproduce). Ruled out by inspection: DirEntry_from_posix_info (clean), ScandirIterator_iternext (clean), DirEntry_fetch_stat/path_object_error (do not touch name/path refs and are not called in the minimal repro; is_dir/stat not needed). PRODUCER PINNED via ASan free-stack (a --with-pymalloc + ASan build at ~/projects/3.16_debug_asan_pymalloc/python, run with PYTHONMALLOC=malloc so frees route through ASan -> heap-use-after-free report): the victim is a DirEntry path string allocated at DirEntry_from_posix_info:16747 (unicode_decode_utf8 <- ScandirIterator_iternext:16923 <- list_extend, building list(os.scandir(d))); it is FREED by PyStackRef_XCLOSE@pycore_stackref.h:726 <- _PyEval_EvalFrameDefault@generated_cases.c.h:13857, i.e. the exception_unwind handler popping+XCLOSEing every value-stack slot when the OOM MemoryError is unhandled in that frame; the later DirEntry_dealloc:16200 reads the dangling field (the USE). NOT a posixmodule bug: DirEntry.name/path are Py_T_OBJECT_EX members and __fspath__ uses Py_NewRef, so e.path is a properly owned ref -- it is an eval-loop stackref-lifetime bug (a value-stack slot to the live string is over-closed; effectively unbacked/double-owned stackref, cf. the stack spill/reload machinery / GH-150516). SAME CLASS as OOM-0005 (PyStackRef_XCLOSE over-close of a value-stack ref under OOM) but distinct site (exception_unwind value-stack pop vs _PyFrame_ClearLocals) and distinct independently-reproducible trigger; possibly the same underlying bug, kept separate pending an rr trace. RESIDUAL: the exact push/spill leaving the slot unbacked is not pinned to a line -- needs rr reverse-exec, which was blocked on the triage host by kernel.perf_event_paranoid=4 (needs sudo sysctl kernel.perf_event_paranoid=3). The earlier --without-pymalloc ASan build rejects PYTHONMALLOC=malloc (WITH_PYMALLOC=0), which is why this needed the dedicated pymalloc build. Dedup key = DirEntry_dealloc@posixmodule.c:16199 (the deduper resolves to it after skipping refcount.h/object.c:275 detector frames). NOT yet gisted -- umbrella python/cpython#151763 already filed with 35 bugs; adding this 36th outward-facing is a separate gated step.
